meaning: n.希腊语字母第 9位,极微小
examples: I don't think that would help one iota.
He's never shown an iota of interest in any kind of work.
The future cardinal was convinced, even then, that he had not abandoned one iota of his Jewishness.
And in none of those cases did the plight of workers improve one iota afterward.
What seemed to really rile people was the council did not shift one iota.
